level_4_sections.summary: ## Actions Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) application programming interface (API). You can use any toolkit that supports HTTP to use the REST API. The REST API uses the standard HTTP headers and status codes, so that standard browsers and toolkits work as expected. Amazon S3 supports the REST API. However, new Amazon S3 features will not be supported for SOAP. We recommend that you use either this REST API or the AWS SDKs at the following link: Headers - Common request headers and Common response headers. Error responses. Requests to Amazon S3 can be authenticated or anonymous. Using Amazon S3 with the AWS Command Line Interface in the AWS Command Line Interface Making direct REST API calls The PUT request header is limited to 8 KB in size. If you'd like to make your own REST API calls instead of using one of the above alternatives, there are some things to keep in mind. After Amazon S3 begins processing the request, it sends an HTTP response header that specifies a 200 OK response. While processing is in progress, Amazon S3 periodically sends white space characters to keep the connection from timing out. Alternatively, for objects accessed through Amazon S3 on Outposts, specify the ARN of the object as accessed in the format arn:aws:s3-outposts:<Region>:<account- reports/january.pdf through outpost my-outpost owned by account 123456789012 west-2:123456789012:outpost/my-outpost/object/reports/january.pdf. If the current version is a delete marker, Amazon S3 behaves as if the object was deleted. If you don't specify a version ID, Amazon S3 copies the latest version of the source object. If you enable versioning on the destination bucket, Amazon S3 generates a unique version ID for the copied object. x-amz-copy-source-server-side-encryption-customer-key Specifies the customer-provided encryption key for Amazon S3 to use to decrypt the source object. x-amz-copy-source-server-side-encryption-customer-key-MD5 If the source object for the copy is stored in Amazon S3 using SSE-C, you must provide the necessary encryption information in your request so that Amazon S3 can decrypt the object for copying.
